evals.name: simple-architecture-diagram
evals.prompt: Create an excalidraw diagram showing a simple 3-tier architecture: Web Browser -> API Server -> Database. Use appropriate shapes and colors from the color palette.
evals.expected_output: A valid .excalidraw JSON file with at least 3 shapes, 2 arrows, and text labels

assertions.text: Has at least 6 elements (3 shapes + text labels)
Uses colors from the palette (not random hex)

evals.name: fan-out-pattern
evals.prompt: Create an excalidraw diagram showing a fan-out pattern: a central 'Event Bus' node with arrows radiating out to 5 consumer services (Email, SMS, Push, Webhook, Analytics). Use the Start/Trigger color for the event bus.
evals.expected_output: A .excalidraw file with 1 central element and 5+ arrows fanning out

assertions.text: Contains all 5 service names in text

evals.name: container-binding-correctness
evals.prompt: Create an excalidraw diagram with 3 labeled rectangles: 'Frontend', 'Backend', 'Database'. Each must have text centered inside. Connect them left to right with arrows.
evals.expected_output: A .excalidraw file where text elements have containerId pointing to their parent shapes

evals.name: section-by-section-large-diagram
evals.prompt: Create an excalidraw diagram of a CI/CD pipeline with 4 sections: Source (GitHub PR), Build (Docker + tests), Deploy (staging then prod), and Monitor (Grafana + alerts). This should be comprehensive with at least 12 elements total.
evals.expected_output: A .excalidraw file built section-by-section with descriptive IDs and 12+ elements

assertions.text: No overlapping x coordinates for major shapes

evals.name: render-produces-png
evals.prompt: Create a simple excalidraw diagram with 2 rectangles connected by an arrow, then render it to PNG using the render script.
evals.expected_output: Both a .excalidraw JSON file and a rendered .png file
